purpose: PostToolUse hook on Edit|Write. Non-blocking nudge that reminds workers to update their progress file after editing/creating project files. Fires only when (a) the changed file is under {project_root} (not a Synapse internal file under tracker_root or hidden config dirs), (b) at least one in_progress progress file exists across all dashboards, and (c) NO progress file was modified in the last 1 minute (avoids nagging diligent workers). Emits `{message: ...}` with the specific filename and the exact JSON snippet to add to files_changed[]. The action ('created/modified' for Write, 'modified' for Edit) is included so the worker can copy-paste.
gotchas: Skip guards: tracker_root, .claude/, .git/, .synapse/ — won't nag for Synapse-internal edits
Uses `find -mmin -1` to detect recent progress activity — depends on filesystem mtime accuracy
Iterates all dashboards/ progress dirs every Edit/Write — O(N) over progress files; small N in practice
PostToolUse cannot block — agent already wrote; this is a coaching message
patterns: Coaching-style PostToolUse: emits constructive message with the exact correction
Activity throttling: only nudge if no recent progress file activity (within 1 min)
conventions: Message includes emoji prefix and the literal JSON snippet to paste
